audit_items.proof_trace: In the 'E' option handler, the return value of realloc() is assigned directly to lud.lud_exts without a NULL check, then lud.lud_exts[nexts++] is dereferenced unconditionally on the next line. If realloc fails and returns NULL, this results in a null pointer dereference (SIGSEGV). All other pointer dereferences in the file are properly guarded; this is the sole unguarded case.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker invoking ldapurl with many -E extension arguments on a memory-constrained system can trigger the realloc failure path, causing ldapurl to crash (DoS via null pointer dereference at L290).
